title: Roku Q2 2024 Earnings Summary
text: Roku significantly improved its financial performance in the second quarter of 2024, with shares climbing over 6% in after-hours trading following the announcement. The company slashed its net loss to $33.95 million, representing a 68% reduction from the $107.6 million loss in the comparable year-ago period. Earnings per share came in at a loss of 24 cents, beating analyst expectations of a 45-cent loss. Revenue reached $968 million, surpassing Wall Street's forecast of $935.29 million and reflecting 14% year-over-year growth. Streaming households expanded to 83.6 million, up 2 million sequentially and 14% annually. Platform revenue grew 11% year-over-year to $824 million, while devices revenue surged 39% to $143.8 million, driven by expanded retail distribution of Roku-branded televisions. The platform achieved its fourth consecutive quarter of positive adjusted EBITDA and free cash flow, generating $43.6 million and $317.9 million respectively. Roku reported "distinct advantages over the streaming services that compete" on its platform through its unique assets and content offerings. The company emphasized growth in sports programming, with streaming hours from its sports experience tripling year-over-year. For Q3 2024, Roku projects 11% revenue growth to $1.01 billion.
